This and the following verse are in metre.]
The Lord of Gods i.e. Indra, put the army of the enemies to
death in battle with the help of the banner, which was erected
aloft, bedecked with wreaths of little bells, and which bore garlands,
umbrellas, bells and ornaments.

Thereafter once Indra gave a bamboo flagstaff to the king of

346 Brhat Samhita
Ccdi, named Uparicaravasu (who could travel to Heaven from the
earth). The king worshipped that according to the sacred laws.
[For the story of Uparicaravasu see the Mahnbhnrata I, 64.
V. 16-17.]

Being pleased with the worship, Indra spoke thus : "Those
kings who emulate Vasu will be blessed with wealth and will have
their orders obeyed implicitly on earth. Their subjects also will be
happy, free from fear and diseases, and have plenty of food. The
